title: Get Creative Features Response
description: Response payload for the get_creative_features task. Returns scored feature values from the governance agent's evaluation of the submitted creative manifest.

results.description: Feature values for the evaluated creative

items.title: Creative Feature Result
items.description: A single feature evaluation result for a creative. Uses the same value structure as property-feature-value (value, confidence, expires_at, etc.).

feature_id.description: The feature that was evaluated (e.g., 'auto_redirect', 'brand_consistency'). Features prefixed with 'registry:' reference standardized policies from the shared policy registry (e.g., 'registry:eu_ai_act_article_50'). Unprefixed feature IDs are agent-defined.

value.description: The feature value. Type depends on feature definition: boolean for binary, number for quantitative, string for categorical.

unit.description: Unit of measurement for quantitative values (e.g., 'percentage', 'score')

expires_at.description: When this evaluation expires and should be refreshed

methodology_version.description: Version of the methodology used to evaluate this feature

details.description: Additional vendor-specific details about this evaluation

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

detail_url.description: URL to the vendor's full assessment report. The vendor controls what information is disclosed and access control.

pricing_option_id.description: Which rate card pricing option was applied for this evaluation. Present when the governance agent charges for evaluations and account was provided in the request.

vendor_cost.description: Cost incurred for this evaluation, denominated in currency.

consumption.title: Creative Consumption
consumption.description: Structured consumption details for this evaluation. Informational — lets the buyer verify that vendor_cost is consistent with the rate card. vendor_cost is the billing source of truth.

tokens.description: LLM or generation tokens consumed during creative generation.

images_generated.description: Number of images produced during generation.

renders.description: Number of render passes performed (video, animation).

duration_seconds.description: Processing time billed, in seconds. For compute-time pricing models.

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

items.title: Error
items.description: Standard error structure for task-specific errors and warnings

code.description: Error code for programmatic handling. Standard codes are defined in error-code.json and enable autonomous agent recovery. Sellers MAY use codes not in the standard vocabulary for platform-specific errors; agents MUST handle unknown codes gracefully by falling back to the recovery classification.

field.description: Field path associated with the error (e.g., 'packages[0].targeting')

retry_after.description: Seconds to wait before retrying the operation. Sellers MUST return values between 1 and 3600. Clients MUST clamp values outside this range.

recovery.description: Agent recovery classification. transient: retry after delay (rate limit, service unavailable, timeout). correctable: fix the request and resend (invalid field, budget too low, creative rejected). terminal: requires human action (account suspended, payment required, account not found).

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

_bundled.note: This is a bundled schema with all $ref resolved inline. For the modular version with references, use the parent directory.
